member.office_address: 2369 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0550

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Peters Appropriations Request Form FY27 (Programmatic and Language)
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: The following form is for programmatic and language appropriations requests for Rep. Scott Peters for fiscal year 2027. Guidance on Community Project Funding requests for FY27 will be shared separately. All appropriations requests are subject to an internal review process. Acceptance of a request does not guarantee submission to the Appropriations Committee. This form will remain open for submissions until 6:00pm ET on Friday, March 13, 2026. If you are submitting more than one request, you must fill out a separate form for each. If you have any questions please email Dillon.Cooke2@mail.house.gov or call our office at 202-225-0508.

fields.label: Requesting agency or organization (ex. City of San Diego, etc.)
Point of contact
fields.help_text: Enter the name of the primary contact person for this request.

fields.label: If making multiple requests list the priority (Ex. 1 out of 5)
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is a user-specific decision that cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Is this request being submitted to multiple members?
fields.rationale: The advocacy strategy involves submitting this request to multiple offices, including leads Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu, to build broad bipartisan support for NASA SMD.

fields.label: Appropriations Division Title
fields.help_text: Select the subcommittee with jurisdiction over this request.
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: The NASA Science Mission Directorate is under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Agency
fields.help_text: The federal agency associated with the request.
fields.rationale: NASA is the specified agency for this programmatic request.

fields.label: Account
fields.help_text: The specific federal account associated with the request.
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is the relevant account for the $9 billion request.

fields.label: Type of request
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for an existing agency account (NASA Science), which is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Total funding request amount (in dollars)
fields.rationale: The specific funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate is $9 billion.

fields.label: Amount included in FY26 President's budget (in dollars)
fields.rationale: The FY2026 President's Budget Request for SMD was $3.908 billion.

fields.label: Amount increase/decrease compared to FY26 President's budget (in dollars)
fields.rationale: This represents the increase from the FY2026 request ($3.908B) to the current FY2027 ask ($9B).

fields.label: FY26 appropriated (in dollars)
fields.rationale: The FY2026 enacted/appropriated amount for NASA Science was $7.250 billion per H.R. 6938.

fields.label: Amount requested increase/decrease compared to FY26 appropriated (in dollars)
fields.rationale: The current $9 billion request is a $1.75 billion increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.25 billion.

fields.label: Program description
fields.draft_value:
* NASA SMD executes high-priority robotic exploration based on National Academies Decadal Survey scientific consensus
* Sustains U.S. leadership in space against rising international competition through fundamental discovery and innovation
* Leverages less than 0.3% of the federal budget to drive STEM workforce development and cross-sector technology spinoffs
* Provides critical data for weather forecasting, planetary defense, and understanding complex Earth systems
fields.rationale: Draws from advocacy talking points regarding Decadal Survey priorities, international competition, and economic ROI.

fields.label: How does your funding request impact or benefit California's fiftieth congressional district?
fields.draft_value:
* $47,014,316 in recent NASA Science obligations directly supports high-tech aerospace and research sectors in CA-50
* Supports General Atomics in San Diego via a $42.9M contract for end-to-end space observatory mission execution
* Funds UC San Diego research ($8.6M+ in grants) for global atmospheric monitoring and climate system modeling
* Empowers local small businesses like Quartus Engineering with $4.2M for advanced optics and telescope technologies
fields.rationale: Cites specific CA-50 spending data, including the total $47M obligation and major awards to General Atomics, UCSD, and Quartus Engineering.

fields.label: Language type
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, not a request for specific bill or report language.

fields.label: Proposed language
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request for funding levels; no specific bill or report language is provided in the request context.

fields.label: Language description
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request; no specific language description is applicable given the request context.

fields.label:
Has this language been included in previous fiscal years? If so, please include the fiscal year AND:

Bill Number
Division
Title
Section
Page Number, OR

Bill Number
Report Page Number
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific historical question regarding prior submissions.
